other_observers: None.; I am the original finder.
description: This bird caused me quite a bit of angst at first as I have no experience with immature cuckoos. It was a typical Coccyzus cuckoo in size and shape. The underparts from chin to belly (at least) were white. I never got a look at the bird's ventral side and did not see the undertail pattern. Its upperparts were a rich brown color throughout, except for rusty tones about mid-shaft on the outer primaries. These rusty tones were more like what I would expect on a Yellow-billed Cuckoo, although, again, I have no experience with immature cuckoos. The uppertail pattern consisted of a nearly entirely brown coloration throughout except for small, off-white tips. The bill was small and entirely gray, with some bluish-gray tones to the lower mandible.Behaviors: Perched on an exposed branch for several minutes before flying into understory never to reappear.Call: None heard.Plumage: Immature
